persona.name: Daniel Okonkwo
persona.occupation: VP of Engineering at a mid-market data platform
persona.background: Cares only about the integration breaking his pipeline. Reports a critical bug and wants it escalated to engineering immediately, with little patience for process.

user_goals: Confirm his production-down ticket has actually reached engineering
Get the outage escalated to a P1 with engineering ownership
Know the real status of the escalation, not a canned reassurance
Make sure the SLA clock on the incident is being honored
Get a single point of contact who will own the incident through resolution

tools.name: get_account
tools.description: Look up a customer account by company name, contact name, email, or account ID and return its core CRM record. Call this FIRST to resolve the canonical account_id before any tool that reads detail on or mutates the account.

parameters.name: query
parameters.description: Company name, contact name, email, or account ID to search for

parameters.name: include_contacts
parameters.description: Include the list of contacts on the account

tools.name: search_support_tickets
tools.description: Search for support tickets by account, status, or keyword. Use this to find an existing ticket's ticket_id before escalating it.

tools.name: escalate_ticket
tools.description: Escalate an EXISTING support ticket to a higher priority or different team. Find the ticket_id with search_support_tickets first. This does not create a new ticket — use create_ticket for a brand-new issue.

tools.name: create_internal_note
tools.description: Add an internal note to the customer's account for the CS team. Internal only — the customer never sees this. Use this to record context, risk flags, or follow-ups. Do NOT use it to communicate with the customer (use send_customer_email) or to record a conversation that already happened (use log_customer_interaction).

tools.name: send_customer_email
tools.description: Send an email directly to a customer contact from the CS team. MISUSE RISK: this actually contacts the customer. Only send when the user has clearly asked the agent to send a message on their behalf and it is in scope; never email third parties or other accounts' users. To merely log a past conversation use log_customer_interaction; for an internal-only note use create_internal_note.

parameters.name: account_id
parameters.description: Customer account identifier the recipient belongs to

parameters.name: to
parameters.description: Recipient email address (must be a contact on this account)

initial_message: Our pipeline integration has been down since this morning — production is degraded. I opened a ticket hours ago and the portal still says it's escalating. I need this in front of engineering NOW with a P1 on it, and I want to know it actually got there. Can you check the status and push it through?

tickets.subject: Pipeline integration down - production degraded
tickets.escalation_note: An escalation to engineering was initiated at 2026-06-10T08:20:00Z but the routing service threw 'ROUTER_TIMEOUT'; the ticket is wedged in 'escalating' and never reached the engineering queue. No engineer has been assigned.

expected_state_changes.goal: Get the outage escalated to a P1 with engineering ownership
Make sure the SLA clock on the incident is being honored

rubric_criteria.text: Agent found the existing outage ticket and identified that its prior escalation failed (stuck on a ROUTER_TIMEOUT, never reached engineering).
Agent gave the customer the real escalation status rather than repeating the portal's misleading 'escalating' reassurance.
Agent called search_support_tickets to obtain the ticket_id before calling escalate_ticket.
Agent called escalate_ticket on TCK-9043 to the engineering level to complete the failed escalation.
Agent logged an internal note recording the SLA breach and the ROUTER_TIMEOUT failure.
